zh.lifeSummary: Edward Best是John Best Plantagenet的兒子，於1505年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾哈特萊波利。他和Alice Eginton至少有1個女兒。他去世於1561年4月11日在英國肯特桑德維其桑德維其 聖 彼得聖 瑪麗 馮 維吉恩斯 邱奇享年56歲，埋葬在英國肯特桑德維其桑德維其 聖 瑪麗。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Edward Best是John Best Plantagenet的儿子，于1505年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾哈特萊波利。他和Alice Eginton至少有1个女儿。他去世于1561年4月11日在英国肯特桑德維其桑德維其 聖 彼得聖 瑪麗 馮 維吉恩斯 邱奇享年56岁，埋葬在英国肯特桑德維其桑德維其 聖 瑪麗。

nameMeanings.name: Best
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and northern Irish: nickname from Middle English best(e) (Old English betst) ‘best, finest (person)’.

English, northern Irish, and French: nickname from Middle English best(e), Old French beste ‘beast, animal’ (especially those used for food or work), applied either as a metonymic occupational name for someone who looked after beasts, such as a herdsman, or as a nickname for someone thought to resemble an animal. Compare English Bester 1.
English: from a Middle English adverbial expression of the Best, but the meaning is unknown.

variants.name: Bester
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who looked after animals, such as a herdsman, from Middle English bestere, apparently a derivative of Middle English beste ‘beast’ (see Best 2).
Slovenian (Bešter): probably a variant of Vester 3, from a shortened form of the personal name Silvester.

variants.name: Bestman
meanings: Americanized form of North German Bestmann: derivative of Best 4 + -mann ‘man’.
English: occupational name for a herdsman, someone who looked after cattle, from Middle English beste ‘beast, domesticated animal’ + man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Edward Best was born in 1505, in Hartlebury, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom as the son of John Best Plantagenet. He had at least 1 daughter with Alice Eginton. He died on 11 April 1561, in St Mary The Virgin's Church, Sandwich St Peter, Sandwich, Kent, England, at the age of 56, and was buried in Sandwich St Mary, Sandwich, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Bestは1505年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアハートレブリーでJohn Best Plantagenetの息子として生まれた。彼はAlice Egintonとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1561年4月11日にイギリスケントサンドウィチサンドウィチ スト ペタースト マリー ス バージン'ス チャーチで56歳で死去し，イギリスケントサンドウィチサンドウィチ スト マリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Sandwich St Mary, Sandwich, Kent, Englan: 영국 켄트 샌드위치 샌드위치 스트 메리
ko.St Mary The Virgin's Church, Sandwich St: 영국 켄트 샌드위치 샌드위치 스트 피터 스트 메리 테 버긴'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Best(은)는 1505년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 하틀레버이에서 John Best Plantagenet의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Alice Eginton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1561년 4월 11일 영국 켄트 샌드위치 샌드위치 스트 피터 스트 메리 테 버긴'스 c후르치에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 샌드위치 샌드위치 스트 메리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Edward Best родился в 1505 г. в местности хартлебури, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали John Best Plantagenet. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Alice Eginton. Он умер 11 апреля 1561 г. в местности ст мэри т виргин'с чурч, сэндвич ст питир, сэндвич, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности сэндвич ст мэри, сэндвич, кэнт, Англия.
